mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns fail to materialize in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return over 60 days in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates directly into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation exceeds the federal funds rate, negative real yields compress the equity risk premium and force portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The negative real rate regime produces negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of cases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls indicate a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent signals severe economic stagnation that triggers recession pricing and institutional deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.0% fails to precede negative 20-day returns in >60% of historical instances, or the signal exhibits a positive Rank IC during stagnation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and rising unemployment confirm a synchronized supply-side contraction that forces immediate equity deleveraging, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: The dual deterioration signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.
